castle_tile.definition: Dark green hex tile. When placed, immediately take one free action as if you had a third die of any value.

pasture_tile.definition: Light green hex tile showing an animal (cow, pig, chicken, or sheep). Score VP for the animal; if the same type already exists in that pasture region, those score again too.

ship_tile.definition: Blue hex tile. When placed, take all goods from any one depot and advance on the turn order track.

building_tile.definition: Brown hex tile. Eight types (Warehouse, Carpenter, Church, Market, Boarding House, Bank, City Hall, Watchtower), each with a unique effect when placed. No duplicate types in the same connected brown region.

knowledge_tile.definition: Yellow hex tile granting an ongoing ability or end-game scoring bonus. 26 different types exist.

mine_tile.definition: Grey hex tile. No immediate effect, but earns 1 Silverling at the end of every phase for the rest of the game.

black_depot.definition: Central depot on the main board. Once per turn (in addition to 2 dice actions), spend 2 Silverlings to buy any one tile from it — no die needed.

depot.definition: One of the 6 numbered sections on the main board (numbered 1–6) that hold hex tiles available for drafting. A die matching the depot number is needed to take a tile from it.

estate.definition: Your personal board with colored hex spaces. Tiles are placed here to score points by completing regions.

silverling.definition: The game's currency. Used to buy tiles from the black depot (costs 2). Earned from selling goods and mine income. Worth 1 VP at game end.

workers.definition: Spent to adjust any die result by +1 or −1, wrapping around (1↔6). Multiple workers can be spent on the same die. Pairs of leftover workers are worth 1 VP at game end.

region.definition: A connected group of same-colored spaces on the estate board. Completing a region (filling all spaces) scores VP based on size and the current phase.

phase.definition: The game has 5 phases (A–E), each consisting of 5 rounds. New tiles and goods are placed at the start of each phase. The phase bonus for completing regions decreases from 10 VP (A) to 2 VP (E).

goods.definition: Round tiles collected via ships. Sold using a die action matching the goods space number. Selling all goods of one type earns Silverlings and VP. Leftover goods are worth 1 VP each at game end.
